background: The research department of a steel manufacturer suspects that one of the company's rolling machines is producing steel sheets with varying thickness. The thickness is modeled as a uniform random variable between 150 and 200 millimeters. Sheets with a thickness less than 160 millimeters are considered unacceptable and must be scrapped.

task.query: Calculate and interpret the mean and standard deviation of $x$, the thickness of the sheets produced by this machine.
task.answer:
To calculate the mean and standard deviation for $x$, we substitute 150 and 200 millimeters for $c$ and $d$, respectively, in the formulas for uniform random variables. Thus,

$$
\mu=\frac{c+d}{2}=\frac{150+200}{2}=175 \ \text { millimeters }
$$
and

$$
\sigma=\frac{d-c}{\sqrt{12}}=\frac{200-150}{\sqrt{12}}=\frac{50}{3.464}=14.43 \ \text { millimeters }
$$

Our interpretations follow:
The average thickness of all manufactured steel sheets is $\mu=175$ millimeters. From Chebyshev's Rule, we know that at least $75 \%$ of the thickness values, $x$, in the distribution will fall in the interval

$$
\begin{aligned}
\mu \pm 2 \sigma & =175 \pm 2(14.43) \\
& =175 \pm 28.86
\end{aligned}
$$

or between 146.14 and 203.86 millimeters. (This demonstrates, once again, the conservativeness of Chebyshev's Rule because we know that all values of $x$ fall between 150 and 200 millimeters.)

task.query: Graph the probability distribution of $x$, and show the mean on the horizontal axis. Also show 1- and 2-standard-deviation intervals around the mean.
task.answer:
The uniform probability distribution is

$$
f(x)=\frac{1}{d-c}=\frac{1}{200-150}=\frac{1}{50}(150 \leq x \leq 200)
$$

The graph of this function is shown in Figure 4.39. The mean and 1- and 2-standard-deviation intervals around the mean are shown on the horizontal axis.

task.query: Calculate the fraction of steel sheets produced by this machine that have to be scrapped.
task.answer:
To find the fraction of steel sheets produced by the machine that have to be scrapped, we must find the probability that $x$, the thickness, is less than 160 millimeters. As indicated in Figure 4.40, we need to calculate the area under the frequency function $f(x)$ between the points $x=150$ and $x=160$. Therefore, in this case, $a =150$ and $b=160$. Applying the formula in the box, we have

$$
\begin{aligned}
P(x<160) & =P(150<x<160) \\
& =\frac{b-a}{d-c}=\frac{160-150}{200-150}=\frac{10}{50}=\frac{1}{5}=.2
\end{aligned}
$$

That is, $20 \%$ of all the sheets made by this machine must be scrapped.
